figures.caption: FIG. 1. Junction geometry, energy dispersions and contours, and sketch of the spin nonreciprocity. (a) Illustration of the studied heterostructure formed by a superconducting altermagnet (AM) coupled to a nonaltermagnetic normal region N2 (orange) of finite length d, which is connected to a left metallic lead (gray). The superconducting altermagnet appears due to an altermagnet (orange) and proximity induced superconductivity from a conventional swave superconductor (blue). The left lead is subjected to a voltage bias VL, while the chemical potential of the normal region N2 is controlled by a gate voltage VG. The red thick solid (blue thin) arrow denotes the current I↓L (I↑L) predominantly carried by spin-↓ (↑) electrons. (b) Energy dispersions in the three regions of the junction at ky = 0, where the shaded regions in the rightmost panel indicate the partially gapped regions in the superconducting AM. (c) Equal-energy contours in the kx-ky plane for the three regions of the junction at E > 0, while (d) the same at E < 0; here, E lies within the red/blue regions in the rightmost panel of (b), which are partially gapped regions of the superconducting AM. The black curves in (b-d) denote spin-degenerate bands in NL and N2, while blue (red) curves in the rightmost panel indicate the spin split energy bands in the superconducting AM, where quasiparticle states are formed by spin-↑ (↓) electrons paired with spin-↓ (↑) holes. Solid (dashed) arrows in (c,d) denote allowed (blocked) transport channels.

FIG. 2. Gate-controlled local conductance and currents. (a-d) Local conductance and currents at eVG = µ = 50∆ in a junction having dx2−y2 -wave altermagnetism. (a,b) Energy- and momentum-resolved dimensionless conductance gσL for incident spin-↑ and spin-↓ electrons, respectively. (c) Energy-resolved local spectral conductances Gγ L as a function of E. (d) Local spin and charge currents IγL versus eVL, with the right y axis showing the spin polarization PL. In (c,d), γ ∈ {↑, ↓,C, S} denotes the spin-↑, spin-↓, charge, and spin conductances as well as the respective currents. The yellow shaded, unshaded, and red shaded regions in (c,d) correspond to the gapped, partially gapped, and gapless regimes of the BdG spectrum defined by Eq. (5). (e-h): The same as (a-d) but with eVG = 0.5∆. Parameters: µ = t, ∆ = 0.02µ, J = 0.5∆, θJ = 0, d = 5a, tc = 1, κBT = 0.01∆, a = 1, t = 1, and ∆± = (1± 0.5)∆.
